Setlist
   Live Trax Series    Previous | Next 

Released on

Tuesday, September 26, 2006

Artwork

View all artwork (13) Â»
Live Trax Vol. 6: 7.7â7.8.06 - Fenway Park - Boston, MA
This was announced on July 17, 2006 - only 10 days after the second show - by far the quickest turn around from performance to announcement, second place being Live Trax Vol. 49 at 42 days.

In contrast, the 71 days from announcement to release date is the longest period of time of any standard cycle release. Exceptions - Live Trax Vol. 1 (no formal announcement date) and Live Trax Vol. 15 (Big Whiskey pre-order bonus). Both had longer periods from announcement to release, but both were atypical announcements.

It was the first, and one of only two Live Trax releases to contain multiple complete shows (Live Trax Vol. 9).

It was the first in the Live Trax series to be released to stores as opposed to an online exclusive.

It was made available for order (along with Live Trax Vol. 7) on red vinyl through the Warehouse store on November 30, 2020. The vinyl release uses the same cover as the reprinted CD release with the exception of black lettering on the "Live Trax Ribbon" that is usually white.

It was made available for order on black vinyl through the Warehouse store on April 11, 2023.

Official Original DMB Release Notes:
"Dave Matthews Band would like to thank all of the fans who helped make the first half of the 2006 summer tour one of the best tours yet. The band is really looking forward to coming back in late July to finish out this special run, including the August 5-6, 2006 Island Getaway on Randall's Island in NYC.

To commemorate the 2006 summer tour, we are excited to announce that we will be releasing both nights of the Fenway Park shows as the next Live Trax volume! This special 4-CD set will contain every note played, including new songs such as The Idea of You and Canât Stop. Included will be recent revivals The Last Stop and Proudest Monkey, as well as DMBâs rendition of the Fenway Park tradition, Sweet Caroline. Please stay tuned to the website for special Live Trax preorder information and announcements regarding the availability of Fenway event merchandise."

Official DMB Vinyl Release Notes:
Available now for the for time on vinyl, this 180g foil-numbered set contains 8 LPs of stunning red vinyl. Memories of the Dave Matthews Band shows in Fenway Park are certain to burn bright for a long while in the minds of the fortunate fans that were in attendance. Dave Matthews Band was on the top of its game in the historic ballpark performing back-to-back nights of shows exploding with energy.

The special Fenway Live Trax volume will contain every note played during both shows. New songs such as The Idea of You, Canât Stop and Break Free are highlights along with recent revivals The Last Stop and Proudest Monkey. The volume also features DMBâs rendition of the Fenway Park tradition, Sweet Caroline.

Additional highlights of the 7.7 Fenway Park show include the Bartender jam and the high-energy Ants Marching closer. An awesome Donât Drink the Water, old favorite Dancing Nancies and the crowd-pleasing show ender, Two Step are some of the favorites from the 7.8 Fenway Park show.

The color hex code for the Live Trax Vol. 6 ribbon is #d42641.
